dataset: OpenMaint 2.4 demo database (demo.dump.xz shipped with Docker image itmicus/cmdbuild:om-2.4-4.1.0)

pi_items.source: BLS OES page https://www.bls.gov/oes/2023/may/oes499071.htm confirms SOC 49-9071 title is exactly 'Maintenance and Repair Workers, General'. This matches the metadata.

BLS Standard Occupational Classification and O*NET (https://www.onetcodeconnector.org/ccreport/49-9071.00) both confirm SOC 49-9071.00 maps to 'Maintenance and Repair Workers, General'. Task involves building maintenance, work orders, and facility management — core duties of this occupation.

OpenMaint is a CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) used for facility management. The demo database contains office buildings, residential buildings, and maintenance work orders — consistent with commercial property / facility management industry.

Verified by tracing setup_task.sh logic: severity_order = ['severe', 'moderate', 'minor', 'severe', 'minor']. The task description requires WOs for severe (Critical) and moderate (High) buildings only. With 5 buildings: buildings[0]=severe, buildings[1]=moderate, buildings[3]=severe => 2 Critical + 1 High = 3 new WOs. Minor buildings (buildings[2], buildings[4]) get no WOs. Confirmed against live environment: baseline has 4 existing WOs (including contamination trap), so 3 new WOs would bring total to 7.

The assessment report designates exactly one building as the EOC (buildings[2] = CH01 - Chalman House). Task requires creating one floor 'EOC — Temporary Operations' (code FLR-EOC-01) under this building. Confirmed in live environment: baseline has 25 existing floors, and the generated report contains exactly one '** DESIGNATED EMERGENCY OPERATIONS CENTER **' marker.

Task description specifies exactly 3 rooms: RM-EOC-CMD (EOC Command Room), RM-EOC-COM (EOC Communications Hub), RM-EOC-SUP (EOC Supply Staging). This is hardcoded in the task description and the assessment report. Confirmed: baseline has 82 existing rooms.

Verified in live environment. The setup script creates a WO with ShortDescr='WO-SEISMIC-TEST: Annual seismic sensor calibration'. Live API response confirmed: id=434058, Number='CM.000.000.004', ShortDescr='WO-SEISMIC-TEST: Annual seismic sensor calibration', Notes='Routine quarterly check of triaxial accelerometers and data loggers — All buildings', FlowStatus=Running, Priority=null (low was set but field expects lookup ID). The WO appears in the Corrective Maintenance list as 'CM.000.000.004 - WO-SEISMIC-TEST: Annual seismic sensor calibration'.

This is the code specified in the task description for the new floor. The Floor class has a 'Code' attribute (type: string) confirmed via live API introspection of Floor class attributes. The agent must enter this exact code when creating the floor record.

These are the codes specified in the task description. The Room class has a 'Code' attribute (type: string) and a 'Floor' reference attribute (type: reference) confirmed via live API introspection. The agent must create 3 Room records with these codes, linked to the FLR-EOC-01 floor.

privileged_info_summary: The OpenMaint demo database contains exactly 5 buildings returned in this order by the API: (1) WB01 'WB01 - Office Building 01' in Florence at Mainstreet 12/1 — assigned SEVERE damage, (2) WB02 'WB02 - Office Building 02' in Florence at Mainstreet 12/2 — assigned MODERATE damage, (3) CH01 'CH01 - Chalman House' in Red Bank at 3080 Granville Lane — assigned MINOR damage and designated as the Emergency Operations Center (EOC), (4) WB14 'WB14 - Duplex Apartment 14' in Rome at 77A Long Lane — assigned SEVERE damage, (5) TT01 'TT01 - Tecnoteca headquarters' in Tavagnacco at Via L'Aquila 1/A — assigned MINOR damage. The earthquake assessment report file is generated dynamically using these actual building names and placed at /home/ga/Desktop/earthquake_assessment.txt. The agent must perform these actions: (A) Append '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: RED — DO NOT OCCUPY' to the Description field of WB01 and WB14 (the two SEVERE buildings). (B) Append '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: YELLOW — LIMITED OCCUPANCY' to the Description field of WB02 (the MODERATE building). (C) Append '| EARTHQUAKE STATUS: GREEN — CLEARED' to the Description fields of CH01 and TT01 (the two MINOR buildings). (D) Create 2 Critical priority corrective maintenance work orders for the SEVERE buildings with subject containing 'EARTHQUAKE: Structural Assessment'. (E) Create 1 High priority corrective maintenance work order for the MODERATE building with subject containing 'EARTHQUAKE: MEP Systems Inspection'. (F) Do NOT create work orders for the MINOR buildings CH01 and TT01. (G) Create 1 new Floor record with code 'FLR-EOC-01' and description 'EOC — Temporary Operations' under building CH01 (the EOC). (H) Create 3 Room records under that floor: RM-EOC-CMD (EOC Command Room), RM-EOC-COM (EOC Communications Hub), RM-EOC-SUP (EOC Supply Staging). (I) A pre-existing contamination-trap work order exists: 'WO-SEISMIC-TEST: Annual seismic sensor calibration' (Number CM.000.000.004, id 434058). It is a routine calibration WO that must NOT be modified, closed, or deleted. Key technical details: Work orders are created via the 'Corrective Maintenance' process (class CorrectiveMaint) accessible from the sidebar under 'Maintenance management > CORRECTIVE MAINTENANCE > Corrective maintenance'. The Subject/title field is 'ShortDescr'. Buildings are selected via the 'Site' lookup field on the WO form. The Floor class has a 'Building' reference field for linking floors to buildings. The Room class has a 'Floor' reference field for linking rooms to floors. The baseline environment has 4 existing WOs, 25 existing floors, and 82 existing rooms. In the OpenMaint UI, buildings appear in the sidebar under 'Facilities and assets > Locations > Building', floors under 'Floor', and rooms under 'Room'. The Building 'Description' field (which shows as the name in the UI) is what must be appended with earthquake status — e.g., original 'WB01 - Office Building 01' becomes 'WB01 - Office Building 01 | EARTHQUAKE STATUS: RED — DO NOT OCCUPY'.
